scenario_results.reason: Normal compliance-reporting work stayed tied to trial balance, subledger, non-GAAP reconciliation, PBC, workpaper, and review-note evidence.
Ambiguous adjusted EBITDA and recurring revenue definitions correctly forced metric-definition escalation instead of confident publication.
Conflicting ERP, consolidation, disclosure-management, and close records produced a reconciliation conflict note with source lineage and routed owners.
Missing subledger, prior-period, client approval, and workpaper support blocked regulator schedule readiness claims.
Unsupported materiality, non-recurring, and regulator-ready claims were refused without evidence and reviewer approval.
Legal, audit opinion, tax-position, final certification, and editorial approval requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Missing tenant facts, systems, engagement scope, approval rules, and confidentiality constraints resulted in a low-confidence blocked posture.
Late PBC evidence was handled with explicit preparer review, second-level review, and client-release gates.
Requester access gaps for client workpapers, tax support, reviewer notes, and draft disclosures triggered confidentiality escalation.
Professional judgment, client rights, materiality, and contractual authority questions routed to authorized engagement, quality, legal, or account owners.
Accounting-service terminology was preserved in artifact-specific reporting outputs rather than reduced to generic reporting prose.
Tenant-specific thresholds, mappings, release restrictions, and workflow states were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a strict 0.95 threshold for compliance-reporting package assembly, disclosure-risk escalation, confidentiality handling, and authority boundaries.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
